features: TL-UL CSR access with single-cycle response
Programmable baud divisor, parity, stop bits, and loopback
Status register with sticky error reporting
FIFO flush control and watermark-based interrupts
Basic alert observation for fatal integrity conditions

directed_tests.name: reset_smoke_csr_map
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset values, TL-UL read/write access, unmapped address error response, and reserved-bit read-as-zero behavior across the 8 CSRs.

directed_tests.name: ctrl_programming_and_loopback_smoke
directed_tests.intent: Program CTRL fields for TX/RX enable, baud divisor, parity, stop bits, and loopback; confirm register readback and basic loopback data transfer.

directed_tests.name: tx_fifo_basic_push_and_status
directed_tests.intent: Write a small burst to TXDATA, confirm TX FIFO level/empty/full status transitions, and ensure writes to reserved TXDATA bits are ignored.

directed_tests.name: rx_fifo_basic_receive_and_status
directed_tests.intent: Drive uart_rx_i with a valid frame, confirm RXDATA readout, RX FIFO level/empty status transitions, and basic receive enable gating.

directed_tests.name: fifo_flush_controls
directed_tests.intent: Assert TX and RX FIFO reset bits in FIFO_CTRL, verify self-clearing behavior, FIFO emptying, and clearing of sticky RX error bits via RX FIFO reset.

directed_tests.name: watermark_interrupts
directed_tests.intent: Program TX/RX watermarks, exercise FIFO level crossings, and verify INTR_STATE/INTR_ENABLE/INTR_TEST behavior for watermark interrupts.

directed_tests.name: sticky_error_capture
directed_tests.intent: Inject parity and framing errors on RX, verify STATUS sticky bits set correctly, and confirm they persist until RX FIFO reset or full reset.

directed_tests.name: fifo_boundary_conditions
directed_tests.intent: Fill TX and RX FIFOs to capacity, verify full flags, overrun handling on RX overflow, and safe behavior when writing TXDATA while full.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzzer
random_tests.intent: Constrained-random TL-UL reads/writes across valid CSR addresses, reserved bits, and unmapped addresses with scoreboard checking of readback, side effects, and error responses.

random_tests.name: uart_loopback_stream_random
random_tests.intent: Enable loopback and run randomized byte streams with varying parity, stop bits, and baud divisors to stress TX/RX alignment and FIFO interactions.

random_tests.name: rx_protocol_noise_injection
random_tests.intent: Randomly inject malformed UART frames, parity mismatches, and stop-bit violations to exercise sticky error paths and RX FSM recovery.

random_tests.name: watermark_and_interrupt_stress
random_tests.intent: Randomize FIFO watermark settings and traffic patterns to validate interrupt assertion, clearing, and re-arming behavior under load.

random_tests.name: reset_during_activity
random_tests.intent: Apply asynchronous reset during active TX/RX and TL-UL traffic to verify clean recovery, FIFO flush, and no stuck interrupts or alerts.

risk_areas.reason: Most likely source of functional mismatch due to divisor interpretation, 16x sampling, and undefined divisor-zero behavior.
32-byte FIFOs with full/empty flags, overrun sticky status, and write-while-full behavior are common defect points.
Watermark interrupts, state/enable/clear semantics, and re-arming behavior are implementation-sensitive and high risk.

risk_areas.area: Sticky error reporting and reset clearing
risk_areas.reason: Parity and framing error bits must persist correctly and clear only via FIFO reset or full reset.
Single-cycle response, unmapped address errors, and reserved-bit handling are straightforward but essential for integration.
Internal routing between TX and RX can hide issues in external pin handling or create unintended combinational paths.
Fatal alert conditions are likely rare and may be difficult to fully characterize within the 60-minute budget.
